en.name: International Mathematical Olympiad
en.description: The most prestigious international mathematics competition for high school students.

statements.en:
Determine all real numbers $\alpha$ such that, for every positive integer $n$, the integer

$$\lfloor \alpha \rfloor + \lfloor 2\alpha \rfloor + \cdots + \lfloor n\alpha \rfloor$$

is a multiple of $n$.

Find all pairs $(a, b)$ of positive integers such that

$$a^2 + b \mid a^2 b^2.$$

Let $ABC$ be a triangle with incentre $I$ and incircle $\omega$. Let $X$, $Y$, $Z$ be the second points of intersection of $\omega$ with the angle bisectors $AI$, $BI$, $CI$ respectively. Let the tangent to $\omega$ at $X$ meet $BC$ at $P$. Similarly, let the tangent to $\omega$ at $Y$ meet $CA$ at $Q$, and the tangent to $\omega$ at $Z$ meet $AB$ at $R$. Prove that the lines $PA$, $QB$, $RC$ are concurrent.

Determine all composite integers $n > 1$ that satisfy the following property: if $d_1, d_2, \ldots, d_k$ are all the positive divisors of $n$ with $1 = d_1 < d_2 < \cdots < d_k = n$, then $d_i$ divides $d_{i+1} + d_{i+2}$ for every $1 \leq i \leq k - 2$.
